methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
FleetZero is a Series A-stage transportation company founded by Steven Henderson and Mike Carter that has raised $43M to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by a sparse comparable set: while eight private peers operate in the transportation sector (including REGENT Craft, Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, and Harbinger), no public comps exist, and the narrow peer set—filtered by stage, valuation tier, and timing—lacks sufficient datapoints (minimum three required) to support peer-median triangulation.

Consequently, stage-progression methodology is the sole applicable approach. Using total capital raised of $43M as a proxy for post-money Series A valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, the method implies an $86M valuation. This reflects the expected value accretion between financing rounds absent transaction multiples or revenue-based benchmarking.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $73M to $99M, centered on $86M, derived from standard stage-progression parameters. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue or unit-economics data, lack of sufficient same-stage peer comparables to validate the 2.0x multiplier against actual market outcomes in transportation, and no visibility into capital deployment efficiency or product traction. A more robust valuation would require either revenue-based multiples or expansion of the comparable set to adjacent stages or sectors.
